coredata.dc:title: Etching and anti-etching strategy for sensitive colorimetric sensing of H2O2 and biothiols based on silver/carbon nanomaterial
coredata.dc:creator: Hou, Wenli
Liu, Xiaoying
Lu, Qiujun
Liu, Meiling
Zhang, Youyu
Yao, Shouzhuo
coredata.dc:description: In this paper, the colorimetric sensing of H2O2 related molecules and biothiols based on etching and anti-etching strategy was firstly proposed. Ag/carbon nanocomposite (Ag/C NC) was served as the sensing nanoprobe, which was synthesized via carbon dots (C-dots) as the reductant and stabilizer. The characteristic surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorbance of Ag nanoparticles (AgNPs) was sensitive to the amount of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2). It exhibited strong optical responses to H2O2 with the solution colour changing from yellow to nearly colourless, which is resulted from the etching of Ag by H2O2. The sensing platform was further extended to detect H2O2 related molecules such as lactate in coupling with the specific catalysis oxidation of L-lactate by lactate oxidase (LOx) and formation of H2O2. It provides wide linear range for detecting H2O2 in 0.1–80μM and 80–220μM with the detection limit as low as 0.03μM (S/N=3). In the presence of biothiols, the etching from the H2O2 can be hampered. Other biothiols exhibit anti-etching effects well. The strategy works well in detecting of typical biothiols including cysteine (Cys), homocysteine (Hcy) and glutathione (GSH). Thus, a simple colorimetric strategy for sensitive detection of H2O2 and biothiols is proposed. It is believed that the colorimetric sensor based on etching and anti-etching strategy can be applied in other systems in chemical and biosensing areas.

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis and characterization of Ag/C NC
Procedure for colorimetric detection of H2O2 and lactate
L-lactate and biothiols sensing in human serum
Possible mechanism of the colorimetric sensor
Selectivity of the L-lactate and biothiols detection system
3.6 Detection of L-lactate and Cys in serum samples

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China ( 21645008 , 21305042 , 21375037 ), the Scientific Research Fund of Hunan Provincial Education Department ( 14B116 ), the Science and Technology Department of Hunan Province ( 14JJ4030 ), Key Laboratory of Chemical Biology and Traditional Chinese Medicine Research (Ministry of Education of China), Hunan Normal University ( KLCBTCMR2011-08 ), and the Aid Program for Science and Technology Innovative Research Team in Higher Educational Institutions of Hunan Province .

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Illustration the colorimetric detection of H2O2, L-lactate and Cys based on Ag/C NC.

(A) Absorption spectra of C-dots (a), Ag/C NC (b). (The inset image corresponds to the colorimetric response). (B) TEM image of the as-synthesized C-dots. (The inset corresponds to the size distribution histogram).
(A) XPS survey spectrum of Ag/C NC. High resolution XPS spectra of C 1s (B), and Ag 3d (C).

(A) UV–vis absorption spectra of the Ag/C NC in the presence of different concentrations of H2O2 (10, 40, 120μM). (B) UV–vis absorption spectra of Ag/C NC incubation with different concentrations of Cys (2, 10, 16μM), Hcy (2, 4, 6μM) (C), GSH (0.4, 0.5, 1μM) (D) and then reaction with 100μM H2O2.
TEM images of the Ag/C NC before (A) and after (B) interaction with Cys.

(A) UV–vis absorption spectra of Ag/C NC treated with different concentrations of H2O2; (B) The calibration curves of the absorbance change versus different concentrations of H2O2 within 0.1–220μM (b); (C) UV–vis absorption spectra of Ag/C NC treated with different concentrations of L-lactate in the presence of LOx; (D) The calibration curves of the absorbance change versus different concentrations of L-lactate within 1–900μM (b); (E) UV–vis absorption spectra of Ag/C NC in the presence of different concentrations of Cys and incubation with 100μM H2O2; (F) Linear calibration curve of the absorbance change versus different concentrations of Cys within 0.01–10μM.

(A) Absorbance change at 402nm of Ag/C NC (Ag/C NC-H2O2) in the presence of various amino acids, metal cations, and biological molecules at the concentrations of 100μM except ascorbic acid were 1μM. (B) Absorbance at 402nm of Ag/C NC (Ag/C NCCys-H2O2) in the presence of various amino acids at 0.1μM.
Determination of L-lactate (A) and Cys (B) in blood serum samples separately.

ce:legend.ce:simple-para: *The found concentration was concentration of the samples not diluted.

head.ce:title: Etching and anti-etching strategy for sensitive colorimetric sensing of H2O2 and biothiols based on silver/carbon nanomaterial

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Chemical Biology & Traditional Chinese Medicine Research (Ministry of Education, China), College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Hunan Normal University, Changsha 410081, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Chemical Biology & Traditional Chinese Medicine Research (Ministry of Education, China)
College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Science, Science and technological innovation platform, Hunan Agricultural University, Hunan, Changsha 410128, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this paper, the colorimetric sensing of H2O2 related molecules and biothiols based on etching and anti-etching strategy was firstly proposed. Ag/carbon nanocomposite (Ag/C NC) was served as the sensing nanoprobe, which was synthesized via carbon dots (C-dots) as the reductant and stabilizer. The characteristic surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorbance of Ag nanoparticles (AgNPs) was sensitive to the amount of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2). It exhibited strong optical responses to H2O2 with the solution colour changing from yellow to nearly colourless, which is resulted from the etching of Ag by H2O2. The sensing platform was further extended to detect H2O2 related molecules such as lactate in coupling with the specific catalysis oxidation of L-lactate by lactate oxidase (LOx) and formation of H2O2. It provides wide linear range for detecting H2O2 in 0.1–80μM and 80–220μM with the detection limit as low as 0.03μM (S/N=3). In the presence of biothiols, the etching from the H2O2 can be hampered. Other biothiols exhibit anti-etching effects well. The strategy works well in detecting of typical biothiols including cysteine (Cys), homocysteine (Hcy) and glutathione (GSH). Thus, a simple colorimetric strategy for sensitive detection of H2O2 and biothiols is proposed. It is believed that the colorimetric sensor based on etching and anti-etching strategy can be applied in other systems in chemical and biosensing areas.

ce:section.ce:para: Various of nanomaterials such as carbon nanomaterials [1,2], metallic nanomaterials [3–5], metal oxide nanomaterials [6] are quickly developed in construction of newly chem/biosensing platform for detection of some small biomolecules [7–9], which were widely used in chemistry [10], biology [11], medicine [12] and material fields [13]. Silver based nanomaterials have become a hot topic in chemistry and biosensors owing to their unique optical properties [14], excellent biocompatibility[15] and low cytotoxicity [16]. Usually, in analytical area, the change of surface enhanced Raman absorbance, ultraviolet-visible spectroscopy (UV–vis) and fluorescence of silver nanoparticles (AgNPs) [17], silver nanoprisms (AgNRs) [18] and silver nanoclusters (AgNCs) [19,20] or their nanocomposites [21] were frequently utilized. Among them, the colorimetric sensors based on silver nanomaterials have drawn intensive attention because it is simple and visual. There are mainly two types of colorimetric sensors involved: one is based on the peroxidase-like activity of the Ag based materials [22], the other is based on the etching of silver nanomaterials by some special substance [23]. Many reports have focused on the former strategy, which was used for the detection of H2O2 [24] or its related biomolecules such as glucose [25,26], urine acid [27] and so on. Only a few studies focus on the latter. Based on the etching phenomenon, for example, the determination of H2O2, cyanide [23,27] was developed. Miao’s group demonstrated that the application of silver nanoparticles for nonenzymatic H2O2 assay [24]. Zeng’s group demonstrated the sensitive and specific detection of cyanide using Ag@Au core-shell nanoparticles [28]. However, most of these colorimetric sensors based on etching is used for single-component detection. Moreover, although the etching effect has been proposed, the anti-etching effect was seldom reported. Therefore, it is possible to utilize the etching and anti-etching strategy to continuously determinate two components, which will show great prospect in future analytical area.

H2O2 and its related molecules and biothiols attract much attention in recent years. H2O2 has been widely used in biology, environmental, chemical and industry fields [29–33]. It is known that, H2O2 is closely related to the metabolism in biological system and it can serve as antibacterial in pharmaceutical field [33]. After all, the use of H2O2 is far more than above. Thus, it is urgent to develop simple and sensitive methods for the detection of H2O2. Meanwhile, various methods were development for the detection of H2O2, such as colorimetric [34], fluorescent [35] and electrochemical [36,37] methods. On the other hand, biothiols, such as cysteine (Cys), homocysteine (Hcy) and glutathione (GSH), are natural sulfur-containing amino acid, which are important components for many systems. Especially, biothiols play important roles in biological systems for their function of antioxidant defence [38]. It is noticed that Cys is present in blood at the highest concentration among the main three biothiols in human body. Cys deficiency may induce many disease, e.g., slowing down the growth in children and depigmentation of hair [39]. However, high level of Cys also caused neurotoxicity [40]. Many reports about the detection of Cys are complicated or time consuming, such as high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) [41], spectrofluorimetry [42,43], chemiluminescence [44,45], electrochemical methods [39,46]. However, colorimetric method is developed as a simple, sensitive and easy method for biothiols, which can also be used for detection of H2O2 or other small molecules.

In this context, a simple and sensitive sensor for colorimetric detection of H2O2 and biothiols via the etching and anti-etching approach was firstly proposed as Scheme 1 . The sensing nanoprobe of carbon-based Ag nanocomposite (Ag/C NC) was synthesized via a facile green reduction strategy using C-dots as both the reductant and stabilizing agent. H2O2 can act as the etching agent to make the characteristic surface plasmon resonance (SPR) absorbance of Ag nanoparticles (AgNPs) decline with the colour change of the solution from yellow to colourless, which may be used for the quantification of H2O2. Combining with the specific catalytic effect of lactate oxidase (LOx) for the oxidation of L-lactate and formation of H2O2, the Ag/C NC can also be exploited as the sensing platform for L-lactate detection via the etching of Ag/C NC by the resulted H2O2. While in the presence of trace biothiols, Ag/C NC cannot easily be etched by H2O2, since the biothiols may serve as the capping agents for AgNPs and prevent the attack from H2O2 [47]. The decline of the absorbance peak of Ag caused by H2O2 was slowed down in the presence of the biothiols. It is interesting that anti-etching result from Cys exhibit good performance. Based on this concept, a simple colorimetric strategy for sensitive detection of H2O2 and biothiols was firstly proposed. Benefiting from the simple and outstanding optical response, good linear range with high sensitivity and low detection limit were obtained. This work will provide a general way for small molecule detection on the basis of etching and anti-etching, which may be widely used in analytical chemistry and biosensing areas.

Sodium hydroxide (NaOH), ethylene glycol (C2H6O2), platinum sheet (Pt), concentrated hydrochloric acid (HCl), silver nitrate (AgNO3), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2, 30%) were obtained from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co. Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Cys was obtained from Tianjin Kermel Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. Sodium L-lactate, LOx, Hcy and GSH were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (St. Louis, USA). Phosphate buffer saline (PBS) was prepared by 10mM Na2HPO4 and 10mM NaH2PO4. All reagents were used directly without further purification. All aqueous solutions were prepared with Milli-Q water.

The electrolysis of C-dots was synthesized with the DC POWER SUPPLY PS-305DM. UV–vis spectra were conducted on the UV-2450 spectra-photometer (Shimadzu, Japan). FT-IR spectra (IR) were collected on a Nexus-670 Fourier transform-infrared spectrophotometer (Nicolet Instrument Co, USA) with the KBr pressed pellet transmission mode. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and high resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) images were collected on a JEM-1230 transmission electronic microscope (JEOL, Japan). X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was carried out on the K-Alpha 1063 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, England). Dynamic light scattering (DLS, Malvern, England) was used to characterize the size distribution of the nanomaterials. The pH values were measured on a pH S-3C digital acidometer (PR China).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterization of Ag/C NC
ce:section.ce:para: The hydroxyl-rich C-dots were fabricated by one-pot electrochemical carbonization of ethylene glycol according to the reported method [48]. The preparation of Ag/C NC was as follows: Firstly, 100μL NaOH (0.1M) mixed with 600μL C-dots solution under rapid stirring, then 140μL of aqueous AgNO3 (10μM) was dropped into the mixed solution and heated at 40°C for 3h. The product was collected by centrifugation at 12000rpm at 4°C for 20min and washed several times. The Ag/C NC was diluted with water (1mL) and stored at 4°C for further use. The as synthesized Ag/C NC was then characterized by UV, IR, TEM and XPS.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Procedure for colorimetric detection of H2O2 and lactate
ce:section.ce:para: In a typical assay of H2O2, 100μL of Ag/C NC was mixed with 300μL of 10mM PBS (pH 7.4) and different concentrations of H2O2 (100μL). The mixed solution was shaken and incubated at 37°C for 30min. Then, the UV–vis absorption spectra were recorded.

For the detection of lactate, the lactate solution (40μL) with various concentrations was firstly mixed with 10μL of 1mg/mL LOx in 1.5mL centrifugal tube and incubated at 37°C for 30min. The analysis process was similar to that of the detection of H2O2. The interfering substances with certain concentration and 50μM L-lactate were mixed with Ag/C NC in the presence of LOx separately. Each sample was tested under the same optimized conditions.

50μL of biothiols aqueous solution at different concentrations and 300μL PBS (pH 7.4) were added into the 100μL freshly prepared Ag/C NC and shaken. After 20min, 50μL of H2O2 were added to each of the mixture solution. The mixture was incubated in 37°C water bath for 1h and then the UV–vis spectra were recorded. The interfering substances and 10μM Cys were mixed with Ag/C NC separately to investigate the selectivity of the sensor. Each sample was tested under the same optimized conditions.

ce:section.ce:section-title: L-lactate and biothiols sensing in human serum
ce:section.ce:para: Human serum samples were collected from Hospital of Hunan Normal University. Serum samples were first treated by ultra filtration with 5kDa in the amicon cell, and then centrifuged at 13000rpm for 20min, the supernatant was transferred into the calibrated test tubes. All serum samples were 20-fold diluted by PBS (10mM, pH 7.4) before adding into the Ag/C NC-l-lactate sensing system. 100μL diluted serum samples and 100μL-lactate of different concentrations were firstly mixed with 10μL 1mg/mL LOx and incubated for 30min at 30°C. The following steps are similar to that of detection of L-lactate.

For the detection of biothiols, 100μL diluted serum samples with 50μL Cys of different concentrations were firstly mixed with Ag/C NC and incubated for 20min. The following steps are similar to those of detection of Cys.

Fig. 1 A shows the UV–vis spectra of the as prepared C-dots and Ag/C NC. In the UV–vis spectrum of C-dots, the absorption peak at 243nm is attributed to the π-π* transitions [49]. When Ag/C NC was conducted, a sharp peak at 402nm exhibited in the UV–vis spectrum can be found which corresponded to the typical SPR absorption peak of Ag nanoparticles. It suggested the formation of the AgNPs [50]. To further investigate the mechanism of the formation of AgNPs in the presence of C-dots, IR spectra of C-dots (a) and Ag/C NC (b) were conducted, and the change of the surface functional groups of C-dots can be characterized, as shown in Fig. S1 in the supporting information. The characteristic peak at 3428cm−1 and 1700cm−1 can be found [48], which are assigned to the peak of hydroxyl groups (-OH) and carbonyl groups (CO) of C-dots. Compared with spectrum of C-dots, the relative intensity of hydroxyl groups (–OH) decreased and the carbonyl group increased which was attributed to the oxidation of C-dots when reduction of AgNO3 in the process.

Fig. 1B shows the TEM images of C-dots. It can be seen that C-dots (Fig. 1B) were mono-dispersed and uniform in size with the average diameter of 2.4nm. XPS measurements were performed to determine the composition and chemical state of the as-prepared Ag/C NC. The XPS survey spectrum in Fig. 2 A reveals the existence of Ag, C and O elements. The Ag 3d3/2 peak at 367.1eV and Ag 3d5/2 peak at 373.1eV (Fig. 2C) show that the slitting of the 3d doublet of Ag was 6.0eV, which agrees well with the theoretic value of the metallic silver [23]. The C 1s (Fig. 2B) shows two distinct peaks at 283.9 and 287.4eV, which were ascribed to the carbon dots [51] and the oxygen containing groups of CO [52], respectively. It is demonstrated that the chemical states of Ag (0) and C were existed in the Ag/C NC.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Possible mechanism of the colorimetric sensor
ce:section.ce:para:
AgNPs exhibit a sharp peak at 402nm in the UV–vis absorption spectrum [53]. With the addition of H2O2, the peak at 402nm declines obviously, which is attributed to the decrease of Ag in Ag/C NC as shown in Fig. 3 A. And the decline extent increased with the concentration of H2O2. Obvious colour change can be observed, which were attributed to the etching of Ag/C NC by H2O2. The mechanism of this study may be as follow [24]:
(1) Ag+H2O2 →Ag+ + OH+OH−
(2)
OH+OH− + H2O2 →O2
− +2 H2O
(3) O2
− + OH+H+ →O2 +H2O

Where H2O2 acts as an oxidant and interacts with Ag/C NC directly and etches Ag/C NC into smaller nanospheres with the formation of Ag ions. Therefore, the Ag/C NC may be utilized as the nanosensor for H2O2 and its related molecules.

However, in the presence of biothiols, such as Cys, the Ag/C NC cannot be easily etched by H2O2. The variations of the absorbance peak of Ag/C NC incubated with H2O2 in the presence of different concentrations of three typical biothiols were shown in Fig. 3B–D. As seen in Fig. 3B, with the increase of the concentrations of Cys, the absorbance peak of Ag/C NC increased and show tiny red shift of the spectra compared with the Cys-free system. That’s because Ag/C NC can be surrounded by Cys through Ag-S bond, then Ag/C NC exhibits tiny aggregated maybe owing to intermolecular hydrogen bonding of Cys. [47]. The colour of Ag/C NC changed from light yellow to yellow. The above results indicated that biothiols have the anti-etching property and can protect the Ag/C NC from being etched by H2O2. The feasible reasons may be as follows: Cys containing thiol and amine groups, which may surround Ag/C NC through interaction between Ag and −SH or −NH2 [54]. Thereby, in the presence of Cys, the etching of Ag/C NC can be hampered when attacked by H2O2 and the extent of etching will decline. This observation provides great potential for quantitative detection of Cys. Therefore, it is supposed that Ag/C NC can be used as the colorimetric sensor for H2O2 related molecules and biothiols.

TEM can be used for further confirmation the mechanism. The TEM images showed that Ag/C NC is about 13nm, and the nanoparticles exhibit good dispersion and surrounded by some smaller C dots illustrated in Fig. 4 A. HRTEM images illustrates that the lattice distance of the Ag is approximately 0.236nm, which is consistent with the Ag (111) plane [55]. Fig. 4B shows the TEM image of the Ag/C NC incubated with Cys. It was found that the morphology of Ag/C NC became slightly aggregate due to the interaction owing to the presence of Cys. The Ag NPs can be protected by Cys and the chance for them to explosion to H2O2 decreased. This result reconfirms the mechanism that Cys can act as an anti-etch agent for Ag/C NC. It is believed that the characteristic absorption peak for Ag/C NC changed dramatically in the absence and presence of different concentrations of H2O2 and different concentrations of Cys and H2O2, which will influence the diameter of the Ag/C NC. To further verify the mechanism, DLS was used to char acterize the hydrodynamic diameter change of Ag/C NC during the reaction processes as shown in Fig. S2. It was found that the average hydrodynamic diameter of Ag/C NC obviously decreased in the DLS experiment in the presence of H2O2, which was probably attributed to the oxidation and etching of the silver of Ag/C NC by H2O2. In the presence of Cys, the average diameter of the Ag/C NC increased to some extent, which may be attributed to the anti-etching of Cys to protect the Ag/C NC. It was found that the hydrodynamic diameter of Ag/C NC in DLS is much larger than that obtained from TEM, which may result from the existence of certain hydrophilic functional groups on the surface of the Ag/C NC and make the hydrodynamic diameter larger [56,57]. All of these results indicated that the Ag/C NC can be utilized for detection of H2O2 and Cys based on the etching and anti-etching strategy.

In order to achieve the best results for H2O2 and Cys detection, the effects such as reaction temperature, pH and reaction time in this system were investigated, respectively. In the detection of H2O2, the results were shown clearly in Figs. S3A and B, the absorption variation reached its maximum at 37°C and pH 7.4, respectively. The absorbance changed sharply at the initial and reached stable state until approximately 30min (Fig. S3C). Herein, we chose 37°C, pH 7.4 and 30min for the optimal reaction conditions in the following experiments for H2O2 sensing. While in the detection of Cys, the incubation time of Cys reaction with Ag/C NC should be considered, as shown in Fig. S4, the absorption variation became almost constant within 30min. Then, with the addition of H2O2, the absorption variation was the highest for the experiment at 37°C and at pH 7.4 (Fig. S3D–E), respectively. The absorption variation increased with the incubation time, and became almost constant when the incubation time was 60min (Fig. S3F), which was chosen as the optimal reaction time for the following study.

The detection of H2O2 and biothiols was performed under the optimized condition based on Ag/C NC using UV–vis spectra. In the system of Ag/C NC-H2O2, it was found that the colour variation of the system was dependent on the H2O2 concentration. As we know, L-lactate can be specifically oxidized by LOx in the presence of O2 to form H2O2, therefore, this system can also be used in detection of related molecules, such as L-lactate. For further quantitative detection of H2O2 and L-lactate, UV–vis absorbance spectra were monitored in the presence of different concentrations of H2O2 or L-lactate, respectively.

As shown in Fig. 5 A, it was found that the maximum absorbance peak intensity of the system decreased with the concentrations of H2O2 from 0.1-220 mM. The maximum absorbance was proportional to H2O2 concentration, providing two linear detection range from 0.1 to 80mM and 80–220mM with the linear regression equation (Fig. 5B) were A=0.0031[H2O2]+0.0367 (R=0.9931) and A=0.0008[H2O2]+0.2010 (R=0.9932), respectively. The detection limit is 0.03μM (S/N=3). Comparison of several previously reported H2O2 sensors by colorimetric with this proposed colorimetric method is shown in Table S1. It indicated that this method is simple, green and facile with wider linear range and lower detect limit.

When detection of L-lactate, it was found that the maximum absorbance peak intensity decreased with the concentrations of L-lactate in the presence of LOx. It provides wide linear detection range from 1 to 30mM and 30–900mM with the detection limit of 0.35μM (S/N=3) for L-lactate detection (Fig. 5C). The regression equation (Fig. 5D) were A=0.0024[L-lactate]+0.0092 (R=0.996) and A=0.0004 [L-lactate] +0.0777 (R=0.998).

UV–vis spectra were also conducted in the presence of various concentrations of Cys with Ag/C NC and 100μM H2O2, as shown in Fig. 5E. It was found that the colour variation of the Ag/C NC from light yellow to yellow with the increase of Cys, the calibration equation (Fig. 5F) was obtained as A=0.0087[Cys]+0.0050 (R=0.997) with the linear range of 0.01–10μM. The detection limit was obtained as 0.004μΜ (S/N=3). Table S2 shows the comparison of several previously reported Cys detection by colorimetric methods with this proposed colorimetric method. It indicated that this method provides wider linear range and lower detect limit. This anti-etching strategy can be expanded to detect of other two kinds of biothiols, Hcy and GSH, as shown in Fig. S5. The experimental results indicated that the platform could also be applied for sensitive assay of Hcy and GSH. The calibration equation was obtained as A=0.0259[HCy]+0.0024 (R=0.994) and A=0.0346[GSH]+0.0005 (R=0.993) with the linear ranges of 0.05–6μM and 0.02–1μM, respectively.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity of the L-lactate and biothiols detection system
ce:section.ce:para: In order to evaluate the selectivity of Ag/C NC-L-lactate and Ag/C NC-biothiols sensor, several potential interferences were examined, respectively. In the system of Ag/C NC-l-lactate, as shown in Fig. 6 , glycine (Gly), tyrosine (Tyr), alanine (Ala), histidine (His), serine (Ser), lysine (Lys), proline (Pro), chloride ion (Cl−), nitrate (NO3 −) at excess concentration (1mM) and ascorbic acid (AA, 50μM) were examined. It was found that only L-lactate (50μM) results in obvious SPR peak decline of Ag/C NC, while the other interfering substances had no evident impact on the determination. In the system of Ag/C NC- H2O2-biothiols, various amine acids such as glycine (Gly), aspartic (Asp), glutamic acid (Glu), alanine (Ala), proline (Pro), lysine (Lys), histidine (His), serine (Ser), threonine (Thr), glutamine (Gin) were also examined at higher concentration (0.1mM). No observable change in the spectra profile was noticed in the presence of these species. In the first step of etching, H2O2 can produce hydroxyl radical that process strong oxidative activity to etch Ag/C NC. AA and DA can not react with Ag/C NC may be the oxidation ability was not strong enough. For the second step, in the presence of Cys, Ag/C NC can be protected by Cys through Ag-S bond, the Ag/C NC cannot be easily etched by H2O2. When AA existed in the system, the AA can not adsorb on the Ag/C NC owing to there was not any special interaction between them. Therefore, the anti-etch was not present in the system and it can not affect the detection.The above results reveal the effective selectivity of the sensing protocol.

ce:section.ce:section-title: 3.6 Detection of L-lactate and Cys in serum samples
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Table 1 , the applicability of the proposed detection system for the assay of L-lactate and Cys was evaluated by analyzing human serum samples. Three samples of blood plasma were analyzed. Standard addition method was used to evaluate the new sensing platform. Different concentrations (0, 10, 50, 150μM) of L-lactate and Cys (0.05, 0.5, 5μM) were added into the sample separately and then diluted with buffer and the results were listed in Table 1. For detection of L-lactate, the recoveries ranged from 96.3 to 107.3% with the relative standard deviation (RSD, n=3) less than 3.2%. The recoveries ranged from 96.5 to 104.1% with the RSD (n=3) less than 3.4% while detecting Cys. These results indicated that the proposed Ag/C NC nanosensor was capable of detecting L-lactate and Cys sensitively in real serum samples.

In summary, a facile and green preparation strategy for stable Ag/C NC was successfully proposed and the sensitive nanosensor for H2O2 and biothiols were developed by the gradual etching of Ag by H2O2 and anti-etching by biothiols based on the Ag/C NC. This can be observed by the change of SPR absorbance intensity of Ag/C NC with the solution colour variation in the presence of different concentration of H2O2. And this colorimetric sensor can also expand to detect L-lactate in the presence of LOx. It is interesting that the anti-etching of Cys exhibit good performance to protect Ag/C NC from attacking from H2O2. Thus, the simple colorimetric strategy by etching and anti-etching proposed here may become a general way for detection of H2O2 related molecules and other biomolecules in both analytical chemistry and biological science. This work will provide a general way for small molecule detection on the basis of etching and anti-etching,

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21645008, 21305042, 21375037), the Scientific Research Fund of Hunan Provincial Education Department (14B116), the Science and Technology Department of Hunan Province (14JJ4030), Key Laboratory of Chemical Biology and Traditional Chinese Medicine Research (Ministry of Education of China), Hunan Normal University (KLCBTCMR2011-08), and the Aid Program for Science and Technology Innovative Research Team in Higher Educational Institutions of Hunan Province.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found, in the online version, at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.colsurfb.2017.11.039.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: The following is Supplementary data to this article:
